hawkish_phrases: risks and vulnerabilities remained, in particular related to geopolitical factors
banks' lower risk tolerance appeared to work somewhat against further improvements in credit provision
recent developments in fixed income markets had prompted a slight deterioration in the conditions of debt financing
balance of risks to the economic outlook for the euro area was seen to remain on the downside
longer-term inflation expectations...were still – across most horizons – well below past levels
dovish_phrases: stand ready to use all available tools within its mandate, if necessary
respond to an unwarranted tightening of the monetary policy stance or a material change in the medium-term outlook for price stability
the recovery in the euro area was expected to remain moderate and gradual, which was considered disappointing
consumer price inflation had remained unusually low
credit growth gradually improving further, although it remained subdued overall
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held rates unchanged (main refinancing at 0.05%, deposit at -0.20%) and maintained its €60 billion per month asset purchase programme through September 2016, signaling readiness to use all tools if unwarranted tightening or material change in the inflation outlook occurs, while viewing the recovery as proceeding as expected but with downside risks remaining.
